findings.message: Standard Bondi-gauge expansion gives g_{uz} = -½ D^z C_{zz} at leading order (see e.g. He–Mitra–Strominger 1503.02981 eq. 2.2), so the ds² contribution is −D^z C_{zz} du dz. The dossier carries a + sign, which is a sign flip on both the dz and d\bar{z} cross-terms.

qa.answer:
The poles follow directly from the IR behavior of the integrand. Split the Mellin integral at some finite $\omega_0$; the UV piece is analytic, so all poles come from the lower limit. If the amplitude (or mode operator) admits a soft expansion

$$a(\omega) \;\sim\; \sum_{n\geq n_{\min}} a_n\,\omega^n \quad (\omega\to 0),$$

then $\int_0^{\omega_0} d\omega\,\omega^{\Delta-1}\cdot\omega^n = \omega_0^{\Delta+n}/(\Delta+n)$, giving a simple pole at $\Delta = -n$ for each term present. For a graviton, Weinberg's soft theorem forces $a(\omega)$ to start at $\omega^{-1}$, so the series of poles runs $\Delta = 1, 0, -1, -2,\ldots$ — exactly the conformally soft points your text mentions at the end of the dossier excerpt.

**Physically**, each pole is a soft theorem in disguise. Taking $\text{Res}_{\Delta=1-n}\,\mathcal{O}_\Delta$ isolates the $\omega^{n-1}$ mode of $a$:

| pole | soft mode | symmetry |
|------|-----------|----------|
| $\Delta=1$ | $\omega^{-1}$ (leading) | supertranslations / Weinberg |
| $\Delta=0$ | $\omega^{0}$ (subleading) | superrotations / Virasoro |
| $\Delta=-1$ | $\omega^{+1}$ (sub-subleading) | $w_{1+\infty}$ charges |

So the poles are not pathologies — they are the dictionary between the $\omega$-expansion of soft factors and the $\Delta$-plane. The Mellin transform converts "take $\omega\to 0$ at order $n$" into "take the residue at $\Delta=1-n$," and each residue operator is the celestial avatar of the corresponding asymptotic symmetry generator.

The argument has three steps.

**1. Split the charge and expand the Ward identity.**
Write $Q_f = Q_f^{\text{soft}} + Q_f^{\text{hard}}$ and expand
$$\langle\text{out}|(Q_f^+ S - S Q_f^-)|\text{in}\rangle = 0$$
into a soft piece and a hard piece. Rearranging:
$$\langle\text{out}|Q_f^{\text{soft}} S|\text{in}\rangle = -\langle\text{out}|(Q_f^{\text{hard},+} S - S\,Q_f^{\text{hard},-})|\text{in}\rangle.$$

**2. Hard piece → Weinberg pole (right-hand side of eq 2).**
$Q_f^{\text{hard}}$ generates a supertranslation $u \mapsto u + f(z,\bar z)$ on asymptotic particle data. Acting on an outgoing massless particle at angle $z_k$ with energy $E_k$, it contributes $f(z_k)E_k$; incoming particles contribute with opposite sign. Summing over all external legs and using the explicit form of the BMS action on momenta, the right-hand side evaluates to
$$\frac{\kappa}{2}\sum_k \frac{\varepsilon_{\mu\nu}p_k^\mu p_k^\nu}{\hat q\cdot p_k}\,\mathcal{M}_n.$$
The factor $\hat q\cdot p_k$ in the denominator comes from the momentum-space form of the BMS generator acting on a plane wave — this is the Weinberg pole.

**3. Soft piece → $\omega\,\mathcal{M}_{n+1}$ (left-hand side of eq 2).**
$Q_f^{\text{soft}}$ is built from the Bondi news: schematically
$$Q_f^{\text{soft}} \sim \int d^2z\;\partial_z f\;\partial_{\bar z}\!\int_{-\infty}^{+\infty} du\;N_{zz},$$
(after integrating by parts in $u$ using $N_{zz}=\partial_u C_{zz}$). The mode expansion of $C_{zz}$ at $\mathscr{I}^+$ contains graviton creation/annihilation operators at all frequencies; the $u$-integral projects onto the $\omega\to 0$ mode. So $\langle\text{out}|Q_f^{\text{soft}}S|\text{in}\rangle$ is the $n+1$-point amplitude with one additional graviton of polarization $\varepsilon_{\mu\nu}$ and energy $\omega\to 0$, i.e.\ $\lim_{\omega\to 0}\,\omega\,\mathcal{M}_{n+1}(\omega\hat q;\{p_k\})$.

**Putting it together.** Equating the two sides gives exactly eq (2). The $\omega$ prefactor on the left cancels the $1/\omega$ that would otherwise diverge, leaving a finite Weinberg residue equal to the momentum-space BMS action on the $n$-point amplitude on the right.

The one subtlety worth flagging: the equality of soft charge insertion and soft graviton insertion relies on the canonical commutation relations for $C_{zz}$ at $\mathscr{I}^+$, which follow from the bulk graviton propagator evaluated near the boundary — if your document works this out in the computation cell section, that's where those $\{C_{zz}, N_{z'z'}\}$ brackets live.

fallback: This is a recorded demo session — try asking about the Mellin poles or the soft theorem, or download TheoryBench to ask about your own paper.
